seoTitle: How to Create a Table of Contents in Canva
metaDescription: Build a clickable table of contents in Canva to help readers navigate reports, ebooks, and presentations more easily.
intro: A clear table of contents makes longer documents feel far more navigable, whether it's a report, proposal, or ebook. This guide covers selecting a template, adding sections, and linking them so readers can jump directly to what they need. It's aimed at students, educators, and business professionals producing structured documents in Canva.

steps.heading: Select a template
steps.body: Choose a document template in Canva that includes or supports a table of contents layout.

steps.heading: Add your sections
steps.body: List out the sections or chapters of your document in the table of contents area.

steps.heading: Customize your design
steps.body: Adjust fonts, spacing, and styling so the table of contents matches the rest of your document.

steps.heading: Enhance navigation with links
steps.body: Add interactive links from each entry to its corresponding section for easier reader navigation.

faq.question: Can a table of contents in Canva be clickable?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva supports adding links, which you can use to make each entry jump to its section.

faq.question: Do clickable links work once exported to PDF?
faq.answer: Interactive links generally carry over to PDF exports, making the table of contents functional for readers.

faq.question: Is this feature useful for short documents too?
faq.answer: It's most valuable for longer documents, but even shorter reports can benefit from a simple navigational structure.
